title: The 60 mV wall
intro: Subthreshold swing (SS) is how many millivolts of gate voltage you need for a 10× change in current — smaller is better. Your GAA device is at 77 mV/dec. Tune gate length and sheet thickness to get under 65 mV/dec. Then try to beat 59 mV/dec. Go on, try.
explain: You hit the Boltzmann limit: kT/q·ln(10) ≈ 59.6 mV/dec at room temperature. It comes from the thermal (Maxwell-Boltzmann) tail of the carrier distribution, not from geometry — so no amount of electrostatic perfection beats it. This is why supply voltage stopped scaling, and it's the fundamental reason leakage vs. performance became a zero-sum fight. Only exotic devices (tunnel FETs, negative-capacitance FETs) can go 'steep-slope' below 60.
